title: AI Model Deprecation Tracker
description: Dated model deprecations, retirements, and shutdown deadlines across tracked providers — text, image, and video APIs — with notice policies and official sources. First-party APIs only; cloud platforms set their own schedules.
citation: Vorp Labs, AI Model Deprecation Tracker (as of 2026-07-15), https://vorplabs.com/models/deprecations

entries.subject: claude-mythos-preview
entries.action: Preview alias retires; move to claude-mythos-5 before the cutoff.

entries.subject: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and claude-opus-4-20250514
entries.action: Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-sonnet-4-6 and claude-opus-4-8 respectively.

entries.subject: claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219
entries.action: Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-sonnet-4-6.

entries.subject: claude-3-haiku-20240307
entries.action: Retired; Anthropic recommends claude-haiku-4-5-20251001.

entries.subject: rerank-english-v2.0 and rerank-multilingual-v2.0
entries.action: Retired; Cohere recommends rerank-v3.5.

entries.subject: mistral-small-2506 and magistral-small-2509
entries.action: Deprecated April 30, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Small 4 as the alternative for both models.

entries.subject: devstral-2512, magistral-medium-2509, and open-mistral-nemo-2407
entries.action: Deprecated May 22, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Medium 3.5 for devstral-2512 and magistral-medium-2509, and Ministral 3 8B for open-mistral-nemo-2407.

entries.subject: mistral-medium-2508 and mistral-medium-2505
entries.action: Deprecated May 22, 2026 in Mistral's legacy table; Mistral lists Mistral Medium 3.5 as the alternative.

entries.subject: kimi-latest
entries.action: Discontinued per the official model list; migrate to kimi-k2.6.

entries.subject: GLM-Z1 series (Air, AirX, FlashX, Flash)
entries.action: The official model overview lists this deprecation date and says deprecated models route automatically; it does not name a replacement for this series.

entries.subject: GLM-4.5-Flash
entries.action: The provider scheduled shutdown for this date with requests automatically routed to GLM-4.7-Flash; the source page still uses future-tense wording.

entries.subject: eleven_monolingual_v1, eleven_multilingual_v1, and scribe_v1
entries.action: A June 2026 notice set this removal date for all three models; for the two TTS v1 models, it superseded the earlier 2025-12-15 date. Migrate to eleven_multilingual_v2 or the current Scribe models.

entries.subject: Legacy model aliases deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner
entries.action: Aliases currently map to deepseek-v4-flash non-thinking and thinking modes; move to explicit v4 model IDs before the cutoff.

entries.subject: claude-opus-4-1-20250805
entries.action: Retirement; recommended replacement is claude-opus-4-8.

entries.subject: July and August 2026 model deprecations (free and developer tiers)
entries.action: Applies to free and developer tiers only; committed-spend enterprise contracts are exempt.

entries.subject: labs-leanstral-1-5
entries.action: Retirement date was set at release; Labs models also carry a training-data exception.

entries.subject: gpt-5-2025-08-07, gpt-5-mini-2025-08-07, gpt-5-nano-2025-08-07, gpt-5-pro-2025-10-06, o3-2025-04-16, o3-pro-2025-06-10
entries.action: Removal from the API; migrate to current GPT-5.5/5.4 model IDs.

entries.subject: gemini-3.1-flash-lite
entries.action: Earliest possible shutdown date per the deprecations table.

entries.subject: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and gemini-3-pro-image-preview
entries.action: Shut down. Migrate image workloads to current model IDs.

entries.subject: kimi-k2 series
entries.action: Discontinued and no longer maintained; kimi-latest was discontinued 2026-01-28.

entries.subject: grok-4-1-fast, grok-4-fast, grok-4-0709, grok-code-fast-1, grok-3, grok-imagine-image-pro
entries.action: Retired; requests to retired slugs auto-redirect to grok-4.3 at grok-4.3 pricing — pin current model IDs to avoid a silent swap.

entries.subject: embed-english-v2.0, embed-english-light-v2.0, embed-multilingual-v2.0, c4ai-aya-expanse-8b, c4ai-aya-vision-8b
entries.action: Retired; recommended replacements include embed-v4.0 and current Command models.

entries.subject: gemini-3-pro-preview
entries.action: Shut down; migrate to gemini-3.1-pro-preview or a stable model.

entries.subject: Sora consumer web and app
entries.action: The consumer Sora product was discontinued; the Sora API remained documented and available past this date, so product and API lifecycles must be tracked separately.

entries.subject: Sora API (sora-2, sora-2-pro)
entries.action: Scheduled discontinuation of the Sora API; migrate video-generation workloads before the cutoff. Rights, retention, and provenance terms for this surface are recorded in the media rights registry.

entries.subject: veo-2.0-generate-001, veo-3.0-generate-001, veo-3.0-fast-generate-001
entries.action: Published shutdown date for the Veo 2 and Veo 3 video model IDs; the pricing page still presented the models after the date passed, so pin veo-3.1 IDs and automate alias checks.

entries.subject: gemini-2.0-flash and gemini-2.0-flash-lite
entries.action: Deprecated and shut down; direct evidence that multimodal understanding models retire on short cycles, not only generation models.

entries.subject: Marengo 2.6 and API v1.2
entries.action: Deprecated; update to Marengo 2.7 and API v1.3. The Generate API rename and the Marengo 2.7 sunset are separate dated events tracked below.

entries.subject: Generate API (renamed to Analyze)
entries.action: Compatibility window for the old Generate API method names ended; applications must call the renamed Analyze API methods.

entries.subject: Marengo 2.7
entries.action: Sunset: indexing, search, and embedding retrieval on 2.7 stopped working. Marengo 2.7 embeddings are not compatible with 3.0, so migration requires reindexing and regenerating all embeddings.

entries.subject: gen4_aleph
entries.action: Official sunset of Gen-4 Aleph; Runway recommends upgrading to Aleph 2.0 (aleph2).

entries.subject: imagen-4.0-generate-001, imagen-4.0-ultra-generate-001, imagen-4.0-fast-generate-001
entries.action: Published shutdown date for the Imagen 4 image model IDs; Google recommends migrating to Gemini 2.5 Flash Image.

noticePolicies.policy: Maintains a legacy-models table with per-model deprecation dates, retirement dates, and recommended alternatives; retirements land in dated waves.

Announces changes in a dated changelog; stable aliases are re-pointed to new models in place, temporary comparison endpoints carry explicit expiry timestamps, and alias retirements are announced with a cutoff date.
Marks discontinued models with dates and a migration target on the official model list; discontinued slugs can remain visible on the page after their date.
Publishes an upcoming-deprecation table with per-model dates and says deprecated model codes automatically route to newer models; a past date alone does not independently confirm shutdown behavior.

Lists deprecated models and removal dates on the models page; the breaking-changes policy promises outreach before endpoint removal but states no fixed minimum notice period, and removal dates have moved.

Lifecycle disclosure is model-specific and inconsistent: gen4_aleph carries a dated sunset in the catalog, other models have carried deprecated badges without dates, and gen3a_turbo was removed from the catalog entirely. A deprecated badge alone does not establish a migration window.

Publishes deprecations in release notes with compatibility deadlines; major model upgrades can require regenerated, incompatible embeddings, so lifecycle cost includes reindexing rather than only a model-string change.
Current Ray API pages coexist with legacy model documentation and a migration guide; documentation drift can leave integrations pinned to superseded model IDs after the commercial product moves on.
Deprecations are announced on a dated page with removal dates; preview models can retire on much shorter notice.
Deprecations are dated with retirement dates and recommended replacements; partner platforms (Bedrock, Vertex) set their own retirement schedules.
Stable models get published earliest-shutdown dates; preview models can be deprecated with at least two weeks notice; -latest aliases hot-swap with two weeks notice.
Retired model slugs automatically redirect to the current model rather than erroring — behavior and pricing change silently unless IDs are pinned.
Deprecated means closed to new customers; existing users (any use within 90 days of the announcement) keep access until the retirement date.
Production models get email notice, a transition period, and a recommended replacement; deprecation exposure depends on contract tier, and preview models can be discontinued at short notice.
Three-day notice for compatible same-lineage redirects, two-week timelines for material changes, and under 24 hours for preview models after 30 days.
At least two weeks advance notice before removing any serverless model.
